message:
fix(ftui-runtime, layout, render, harness): correctness and overflow hardening sweep

Input fairness guard (input_fairness.rs):
- Gate all 3 intervention types (InputLatency, ResizeDominance,
  FairnessIndex) on has_pending_input to prevent spurious triggers
- Always update recent_input_arrival (not just on first since last check)
  for accurate latency stats
- Set pending_input_latency to None when no input is actually pending
- Add epsilon to Jain's index comparison to avoid float boundary issues
- Add 6 new tests covering flood-without-pending and post-processed scenarios

FlakeDetector (flake_detector.rs):
- Track first_flaky_at and max_e_value incrementally as struct fields
  instead of scanning evidence_log in summary()
- Remove unused online_mean/online_m2 Welford's algorithm remnants
- New test: summary_tracks_metrics_when_logging_disabled

DiffEvidenceLedger (diff_evidence.rs):
- Reset current_regime to StableFrame on clear() instead of leaving
  stale value
- New test verifying pre/post-clear regime transition

Posterior probability overflow (unified_evidence.rs):
- Fix NaN from exp(1000) overflow using log-sum-exp trick: branch on
  sign of log_posterior, use 1/(1+exp(-x)) for positive values
- Tests verifying finiteness at +/-1000.0 log-odds

Arithmetic hardening:
- ftui-layout: redistribute_overflow division widened to u64 with
  u16::MAX clamp before cast
- ftui-render/cell.rs: mask GraphemeId generation to 11 bits with
  debug_assert for MAX_GENERATION
- ftui-render/presenter.rs: 3 subtractions changed to saturating_sub,
  2 width computations widened to usize before subtraction
- ftui-harness/resize_storm.rs: clamp resize events to configured
  min/max bounds via clamp_to_bounds() helper
- ftui-runtime/terminal_writer.rs: full-redraw uses buffer.height()
  .min(visible_height) to prevent out-of-bounds diff runs

Test rewrites:
- resize_migration_compat: rewrite burst_cooldown_hysteresis to drive
  cooldown via cfg.cooldown_frames loop instead of fragile timing
- voi_eprocess_unit_tests: formatting fixes in assert messages

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

         !voi_spans.is_empty(),

-        voi_handle.spans().iter().map(|s| &s.name).collect::<Vec<_>>()
+        voi_handle
+            .spans()
+            .iter()
+            .map(|s| &s.name)
+            .collect::<Vec<_>>()
     );
 
     let ep_spans: Vec<_> = ep_handle
@@ -485,7 +489,11 @@ fn both_spans_emitted_in_combined_workflow() {
     assert!(
         !ep_spans.is_empty(),
         "expected eprocess.update span, got spans: {:?}",
-        ep_handle.spans().iter().map(|s| &s.name).collect::<Vec<_>>()
+        ep_handle
+            .spans()
+            .iter()
+            .map(|s| &s.name)
+            .collect::<Vec<_>>()
     );
 
     // Verify key fields on each
